title: Mamata Banerjee loses cool, slaps TMC worker during protest; BJP shares video
content: A video showed Mamata Banerjee slapping a party worker during a protest march in Kolkata. The incident occurred during a rally organized by the TMC's youth wing. Clashes erupted between BJP and TMC supporters along the protest route. Mamata Banerjee accused the police of failing to enforce court orders. The protest was held amid outrage over a minor girl's alleged rape and murder.

title: New USCIS rules for H-1B, Green Card applicants start July 10
content: New United States Citizenship and Immigration Services rules will take effect soon. Applicants must use handwritten or properly reproduced signatures for immigration forms. Improper signatures will lead to rejection without any chance for correction. Applicants should always sign forms by hand before submitting them. This policy aims to maintain the integrity of the immigration system.

title: 'They may target again': Dombivli doctor assaulted by Sena corporator weighs quitting job
content: A young doctor is traumatized after an assault at Shastri Nagar Hospital.  She is now considering quitting her job despite the main accused's arrest.  Her family expressed concerns about her safety and mental well-being following the incident.  The attack on medical staff led to widespread outrage and protests by doctors.  Routine OPD services were suspended while emergency care continued during the protest.

title: Watch: Gaganyaan parachute system in action as Isro tests crew module’s safe splashdown
content: ISRO successfully conducted a key qualification test for Gaganyaan's main parachute system. This test validated the parachute's structural integrity under maximum expected load conditions. A simulated assembly was dropped from an aircraft to test the system's performance. This marks the fifth integrated main parachute airdrop test for the mission. The successful test provides confidence in the parachute system for the upcoming uncrewed mission.

title: Watch: Pinaka long-range rocket roars off launcher in DRDO’s latest test
content: The Defence Research and Development Organisation successfully conducted a flight-test of the Pinaka Long Range Guided Rocket. This successful trial marks another milestone in India's indigenous rocket artillery development. The system is designed to deliver precision strikes at extended ranges for the Army. This latest test follows a successful proof trial of the Pinaka Extended Range Rocket.

title: Meta launches AI image models, company's 'highest-paid employee' shares a 'personal photo' tip
content: Meta launched Muse Image, its first AI image generator from Meta Superintelligence Labs, alongside a preview of Muse Video. Chief AI officer Alexandr Wang, one of the company's highest-paid hires, urged users to keep it personal: upload your own photos, tag friends, and pull from trends. The launch also drew privacy backlash over a feature that uses public Instagram photos by default.

title: What's 1994 'robbery' reference in Argentina's WC anthem Messi sings - Explained
content: Argentina looked on the verge of elimination after falling 2-0 behind in the 67th minute. But the defending champions scored three goals in the final 13 minutes, with Cristian Romero, Lionel Messi and Enzo Fernandez finding the net to complete the turnaround.

title: Space balls twice the size of basketballs wash up on Australian beach
content: Metallic spheres have mysteriously appeared on an Australian beach, sparking local alarm. After thorough investigation, authorities determined the oddities were actually remnants of space debris—specifically pressure vessels from rocket launch vehicles that survived their fiery return to Earth. With the surge in space explorations, experts predict that encounters with such debris will become more frequent as launches increase.

title: Fan spends life savings on World Cup trip for grandfather, then gets money back
content: A grandson spent his life savings to take his grandfather to the World Cup. They became internet famous while travelling across the USA and Mexico. A crypto casino promised to refund the money if England won their match. Following England's victory, the company honored their word, astonishing the young fan. The pair continues to enjoy their memorable journey together at the tournament.

title: Microsoft’s $7.3 billion AI data centre sued by neighbours over nonstop noise
content: Microsoft's latest AI data center in Wisconsin is under fire as residents file a lawsuit claiming the relentless noise from generators and HVAC systems is harming their property. While Microsoft asserts its dedication to being a good neighbor and has taken steps to mitigate noise, the local community remains unimpressed, especially with more data centers on the horizon that may escalate tensions.

title: Baruipur rape-murder probe: Accused sold 12-yr-old to co-accused for Rs 10,000
content: Police encounter death of Prabhas Mondal will not derail the investigation. Mondal confessed to luring the child for Rs 10,000 cash. His disclosures led to the victim's body recovery and other arrests. Forensic evidence and CCTV footage have already been secured. The investigation will continue against the three surviving accused.

title: China will benefit if India-US trade deal doesn’t happen: Economist Surjit Bhalla
content: The trade deal has the potential to boost competitiveness and accelerate India’s journey towards becoming a developed nation, Surjit Bhalla said. However, he warned that prolonged delays in concluding such an agreement would ultimately work to China's advantage.

title: 'Honeymoon suite' inside Indian Railways 1st AC coach goes viral; TTE suspended
content: A viral video showed an Indian Railways First AC cabin transformed into a honeymoon suite. The compartment was decorated with balloons and floral arrangements for a couple. Railway authorities suspended the Traveling Ticket Examiner at Jalna station. An official inquiry has been ordered into the incident which occurred on July 6. The event styling service claimed ignorance about needing official permission.

title: Fans react as Wimbledon celebrates Novak Djokovic and Lionel Messi's legacy
content: Novak Djokovic delivered one of the toughest wins of his career. He survived a five-hour-15-minute battle against Felix Auger-Aliassime in the Wimbledon 2026 quarter-finals. The Serbian star fought through a calf injury and immense pressure to secure a dramatic five-set victory. His remarkable effort booked a blockbuster semi-final clash against world No. 1 Jannik Sinner.

title: Chennai GM Open: Full player list from Gukesh to Niemann, format, prize money, and key details
content: The Chennai Grand Masters 2026 will be held from July 16-22 in Chennai, featuring World Champion Gukesh Dommaraju, Arjun Erigaisi, Hans Niemann and other top grandmasters. The eight-player single round-robin tournament offers a Rs 75 lakh prize pool and crucial FIDE Circuit points. Organisers have confirmed there will be no Challengers section this year.

title: 'Deeply saddened': Jay Shah mourns passing of ex-Afghanistan cricketer Zadran
content: Former Afghanistan fast bowler Shapoor Zadran has died aged 38 after battling the rare immune disorder HLH. The Afghanistan Cricket Board and ICC Chairman Jay Shah paid tribute, hailing him as a pioneer of Afghan cricket. Shah remembered Zadran's match-winning heroics in Afghanistan's first-ever World Cup victory in 2015. He played 80 international matches between 2009 and 2020.

title: 'Asked to stay away for a few days': Gopal Rao denies being removed from Ram Temple trust
content: Gopal Rao stated he was asked to temporarily stay away from trust meetings.  Champat Rai and Anil Mishra resigned from the trust taking moral responsibility.  Most allegations against Rao are false, except for donation theft claims.  Donations are safe and verifiable, with police handling theft arrests.  An SIT continues its investigation into alleged donation misappropriation.

title: Mamata rally turns tense: 'Chor chor' slogans raised, eggs thrown, BJP-TMC workers clash
content: Eggs were allegedly thrown at Mamata Banerjee's protest march in Kolkata on Wednesday. Clashes broke out between Trinamool Congress and BJP workers during the rally. Police resorted to a baton charge to control the escalating situation and restore order. Mamata Banerjee accused the police of acting as an arm of the BJP organization. The Calcutta High Court had previously allowed the protest march under specific conditions.

title: Israeli strike kills Gaza's Argentina-Egypt FIFA WC screening organiser
content: The strike came just before what was expected to be a major occasion for football fans in Gaza, where many had gathered to watch Egypt face defending champions Argentina.

title: Nato chief defends US strikes on Iran, calls them 'absolutely necessary'
content: Nato Secretary General Mark Rutte defended recent US military strikes on Iran. He described the action as absolutely necessary after alleged Iranian attacks on commercial vessels. US forces targeted over eighty sites in response to the maritime incidents. Iran retaliated with drone and missile attacks on US-linked facilities. The events occurred ahead of the 2026 Nato Summit in Ankara.

title: US-Iran war impact: IMF lowers India’s GDP growth forecast marginally
content: According to the IMF, the global economy has so far avoided a steeper slowdown despite the war, as strong demand linked to the technology sector has helped offset the impact of reduced energy supplies caused by the conflict.

title: Building collapses in Delhi's Rohini after rain; several feared trapped
content: An under-construction house collapsed in northwest Delhi's Rohini on Wednesday evening. Rainfall preceded the incident, prompting an immediate rescue operation. Two individuals have been pulled from the debris so far. Rescue efforts are still underway near an MCD school in Sector 16. Police and other emergency teams have cordoned off the affected area.

title: Egypt lodge complaint with FIFA after Argentina loss, seek referee's removal
content: The Egyptian camp was unhappy with several refereeing decisions during the match. These included a goal ruled out after a VAR review and a penalty appeal that was not reviewed in the build-up to Argentina's winning goal.

title: Shiv Sena corporator arrested for assault on doctor, hospital staff in Thane
content: In Thane, Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre faces arrest following an incident where medical staff were assaulted. The altercation erupted when the relatives of a newborn expressed frustration at the hospital. In response, doctors across various hospitals have suspended outpatient services, demanding the apprehension of all involved. Though Mhatre expressed sorrow over the incident, he refuted claims of having assaulted a female doctor.

title: 'I can't look at you, I'm sorry': Argentina coach's post-match interview goes viral - Watch
content: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni broke down in tears after his side's dramatic 3-2 World Cup comeback win over Egypt, walking away from a live TV interview after apologising. Argentina overturned a 2-0 deficit with late goals from Cristian Romero, Lionel Messi and Enzo Fernández. Scaloni later said, "I cried because I'm too emotional," praising his team's fighting spirit.

title: Rs 35 cr horse trading allegations: Madras HC grants anticipatory bail to Senthil Balaji
content: The Madras High Court granted conditional anticipatory bail to DMK MLA V Senthil Balaji and his brother.  This bail is in connection with an alleged Rs 35 crore horse trading attempt.  The attempt aimed to destabilize the ruling TVK government, according to a complaint.  The court directed both petitioners to appear before the investigating officer twice daily.  Their names do not appear in the FIR, petitioners stated, citing political motives.

title: Anchor, 'Jug Jugg Jeeyo' actor Maniesh Paul's mother passes away at 77
content: Actor Maniesh Paul mourns his mother Urmil Paul's passing in Delhi. She was 77 years old and died on July 8, 2026. His team confirmed the news through an official statement issued to the public. Maniesh shared a close bond with his mother throughout his life. Fans are requested to keep her soul in their prayers.

title: NCERT textbook revision: Hitler references dropped, Congress' Partition stand tweaked
content: NCERT has released a revised Class 8 Social Science textbook following a Supreme Court directive. The new edition removes a controversial chapter on the judiciary and alters historical narratives. Revisions include changes to the Congress' stance on the 1947 Partition and the removal of Hitler references. The history chapter now includes Vinayak Damodar Savarkar's demand for Swaraj. This revised book focuses on the judiciary's constitutional role and its structure.

title: Rajesh Sharma rushed to Kolkata hospital with high fever and breathing issues
content: Actor Rajesh Sharma was hospitalized in Kolkata with a severe leg infection and high fever. He developed the condition after an insect bite while filming on Prabhas' movie sets. His condition worsened during his flight to Kolkata, requiring immediate medical attention. Doctors are closely monitoring his health for potential complications and recovery progress. Family and colleagues have expressed gratitude for the widespread support and prayers.

title: Bloodbath on Dalal Street! Sensex, Nifty50 tank 2% - top reasons for sharp fall
content: Stock market crash: Selling pressure gathered pace during the second half of the session after US President Donald Trump declared that the interim agreement with Iran to end the conflict was "over," reigniting fears of further escalation in the Middle East.

title: 'Kept under police watch': Mamata accuses cops of acting like 'BJP cadres'
content: Mamata Banerjee alleged police watch as house arrest and accused the administration of failing to ensure a peaceful protest. The Trinamool Congress chief stated that the police acted as BJP cadres, undermining a court order. She claimed participants were attacked despite court permission for the rally over a minor's alleged rape and murder. Banerjee further alleged a complete breakdown of law and order in West Bengal.

title: Income tax notices decoded: What taxpayers should know about ITR filing to avoid scrutiny
content: One important thing to understand is this: In the current compliance environment, tax notices are increasingly driven by data inconsistencies rather than intent. A tax notice can not only mean a process of explaining discrepancies but can also put any refund claims on hold till the matter is resolved.

title: Bihar sends requisition to fill up 927 teachers’ posts for Backward, Extremely Backward Classes residential schools
content: Bihar government is also working on a proposal to establish 10 Sainik schools for the BC category, says Minister Rama Nishad

title: Wayanad debris slip Live: CM V.D. Satheesan visits the spot; search on for five missing
content: A day after a debris slip at the tunnel road construction site in Kerala's Wayanad district claimed three lives, extensive search operations are ongoing to find five people who are still missing

title: Cancer burden is nearly universal, yet highly inequitable in its impact: WHO report
content: The first such survey by the WHO indicates widening inequities in access to prevention, diagnosis, treatment, and supportive care; while 87% of women with breast cancer survive at five years after their diagnosis in high-income countries, only about 42% do so in low-income countries

title: Police raid ‘Kallapi’ powder manufacturing units amid rising suicide cases across Kadapa district
content: Kallapi powder is a cheaper alternative to cow dung mixed water, which is used by women to sprinkle at the portico of houses; a total of 19 persons allegedly ended their lives in Kadapa district between January 1, 2025, and July 6, 2026, after consuming the powder, say police

title: U.S. students study natural farming practices in Kuppam
content: Academic tour under the APCNF programme gives students first-hand exposure to sustainable farming methods and interactions with cultivators

title: Committee to study implementation of 62-year retirement age in power utilities
content: It is mandated to examine the financial implications, working conditions of employees, and other administrative matters, and submit a report in 15 days

title: Sakhi centres offer vital support to women in distress: Women’s Commission Chairperson
content: Sailaja says trauma-informed care and staff well-being are essential for delivering sensitive, survivor-centred services

title: Watch: U.S.-Iran ceasefire ‘over’: what happens now? | Above the Fold | 08.07.2026
content: From the escalating conflict in West Asia to latest developments in the 2023 killing of a Khalistani separatist in Canada, we bring you the latest in this episode. We also unpack explosive findings from the SIT report on the alleged Ram Temple embezzlement case, and more

title: Endowments Secretary inducted into TTD Board
content: After taking oath as ex officio member, Kannababu, accompanied by his family members, offers prayers to the presiding deity

title: Drip irrigation may replace water tankers to maintain ORR greenery amid road safety concerns
content: Maintenance of greenery on the ORR through deployment of water tankers has claimed several lives in recent years.

title: Political tensions rise over temple hair auction row at Allagadda
content: Former MLA and YSRCP leader Gangula Brijendra Nath Reddy accuses current MLA and TDP leader Bhuma Akhila Priya and her family of allegedly obstructing the transportation of temple hair consignments in order to secure commissions from the contracts

title: 42 defence startups take centre stage at Hyderabad conclave as Telangana bets big on military tech
content: Industry leaders called for faster procurement, stronger funding and greater adoption of homegrown technologies

title: Kerala seeks greater Central support to tackle human-wildlife conflict; Forests Minister Shibu Baby John meets Union Minister Bhupender Yadav
content: While the Centre responded positively to several proposals, it refused to accept the State’s long-standing demand to declare wild boar as vermin, says Minister

title: Jagan pays tributes to YSR on his birth anniversary at Idupulapaya
content: The YSRCP president’s mother Vijayamma and wife Bharathi, and several party leaders are among those who paid their respects to the late leader at his samadhi

title: 'I can touch 160 kph': Ashok Sharma wants to bamboozle batters with pace | Exclusive
content: In the last six months, Ashok Sharma has caught everyone's attention with his raw pace. He shattered an 11-year-old Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy record by picking up 22 wickets for Rajasthan. He impressed everyone in the IPL with his pace for Gujarat Titans. A couple of weeks ago, he was picked for India A as an injury replacement, and now he has earned his maiden India call-up for the Zimbabwe tour, starting on July 23.

title: 16 feared trapped after building collapses in Pune amid heavy rain
content: A three-storey administrative building at the PCMC Waste-to-Energy plant collapsed during heavy rain. Fifteen to sixteen workers are feared trapped under the debris following the afternoon incident. A large-scale rescue operation was immediately launched by the PCMC fire brigade. Personnel from the National Disaster Response Force were also rushed to assist. Rescue operations are currently underway to find those believed to be trapped.

title: YSRCP vows to uphold YSR’s ideals, fight for public welfare
content: The party leaders extol the former Chief Minister’s welfare and development initiatives on his birth anniversary

title: ‘City of Tanks and Lakes’ plan reaches DPR stage in Tirupati
content: TMC and TUDA adopt integrated approach to rejuvenate 23 water bodies and strengthen stormwater management to improve flood resilience

title: Delhi, Mumbai grapple with heavy rain havoc | Monsoon LIVE updates
content: Three-storey under-construction house in Delhi's Rohini collapsed on Wednesday evening, trapping several people; 16 people are feared to be trapped under a collapsed garbage mound in Maharashtra's Pimpri Chinchwad

title: Passport Seva Kendra opened at Thavanur post office
content: The Thavanur POPSK is expected to improve access to passport services for residents of the Ponnani Lok Sabha constituency and neighbouring areas

title: Baruipur rape-murder case: One key accused killed in police encounter
content: Violence had erupted on July 5, when the body of the minor girl was recovered from a pond at Suryapur in Baruipur; it was the first police encounter since BJP came to power in West Bengal

title: Kurnool Collector urges voters to submit SIR forms by July 14
content: Inspecting the digitisation of enumeration forms at Aspari mandal, Siri directs booth-level staff to raise their daily processing target from around 70 forms to at least 150

title: Karnataka plans to appoint a nodal agency to monitor daycare centres
content: Following the recent case of alleged abuse of children at a daycare centre on the campus of IT major Capgemini, the State government plans to regulate daycare centres and appoint a nodal agency for their monitoring

title: Rainfall situation better, kharif sowing improved: Chouhan
content: Agriculture Minister says Centre closely monitoring situation in States; expresses confidence that rainfall will gain further momentum during July

title: Jagan seeks TET exemption for pre-2011 teachers
content: YSRCP chief says making the eligibility test mandatory for teachers appointed before its introduction is unfair and affects their promotions and job security

title: Why did the U.S. indict Lawrence Bishnoi over Hardeep Singh Nijjar’s murder?
content: The U.S. has indicted Lawrence Bishnoi, a jailed Indian gangster, for his alleged role in a transnational conspiracy linked to the murder of Khalistani separatist Hardeep Singh Nijjar in Canada in June 2023.

title: 93 shifted to relief camps after Wayanad debris slip
content: Ministers direct officials to strengthen relief and disaster management measures after the tragedy that claimed three lives, with five persons still missing

title: Andhra Pradesh sets up first overseas investment facilitation centre in Busan
content: APEX Korea will strengthen long-term business partnerships and position Andhra Pradesh as a preferred destination for manufacturing and technology investments, observes HRD Minister Nara Lokesh

title: Bid to incite communal tensions in Andhra Pradesh, alleges BJP State president
content: Madhav accuses certain groups of making derogatory remarks against Hindu deities and attempting forced religious conversions; says development projects are gathering pace under the NDA government

title: 10 Trapped As Collapsing Garbage Mound Takes Building Down With It Near Pune
content: Most of those trapped are believed to be the employees of the private company who were at work inside the building.

title: India, Maldives Begin Talks On Free Trade Agreement To Boost Economic Ties
content: The announcement followed bilateral discussions between Maldives Minister of Economic Development, Transport and Trade Mohamed Saeed and India's Minister of Commerce and Industry, Shri Piyush Goyal, during Minister Saeed's official visit to India.

title: US To Let Ukraine Make Patriot Missiles To Fight Russia, Says Trump
content: Ukraine has been struggling to shoot down Russian ballistic missile attacks as supplies of the crucial US-made Patriot interceptors run low.

title: Trump's Board Of Peace Plans First 'Humanitarian Zone' In South Gaza: Report
content: The zone could act as a "starting point" for the Palestinian technocratic committee meant to assume day-to-day governance during Gaza's transitional phase out of war under Trump's 20-point plan.

title: Video: Mamata Banerjee Slaps Man Amid Trinamool vs BJP At Kolkata Rally
content: The Trinamool Congress rally was regularly interrupted by jostling, pushing, and shoving between workers of the party and the BJP.

title: Pakistan Locates Wreckage Of Missing Cargo Plane In Sea, Search On For Crew
content: The rescue forces, after searching for 12 hours, successfully located and identified wreckage of K2 Airways Cargo B737.

title: India Among Fastest Growing Economies Despite Global Slowdown: IMF
content: The report showed that global growth is projected to slow to 3.0 per cent in 2026 before recovering to 3.4 per cent in 2027.

title: Vijay Visits Children's Hospital In Chennai After Viral Video Alleges Lapses
content: During the visit, the Chief Minister interacted with doctors, reviewed hospital facilities and spoke to mothers admitted to the hospital.

title: India Tests Pinaka Long-Range Guided Rocket With "Textbook Precision"
content: The Pinaka LRGR rocket is designed by Armament Research and Development Establishment in association with High Energy Materials Research Laboratory.

title: "Highlights Inhuman Torture, Sacrifice": Sukhbir Badal Says Will Screen "Satluj"
content: Sukhbir Badal said Satluj "narrates the truth of that painful period which did not stop even after the Congress party's military attack on Sachkhand Sri Harmandir Sahib and the burning alive of thousands of unarmed innocent Sikh youth"

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Trump Says NATO Failed US On Iran
content: Iran War: The United States launched "power strikes" against Iran following a series of attacks by Tehran on commercial ships near the Strait of Hormuz.

title: "Deliberate Fearmongering": Hardeep Puri To NDTV On E20 Row
content: Petroleum Minister told NDTV that the E20 backlash mirrors last year's manufactured LPG panic, and insisted India's biofuel push has saved Rs 1.9 lakh crore in forex and cut import dependence amid Hormuz-linked volatility.

title: "Terrified, Getting Threats": Thane Doctor Assaulted By Sena Corporator
content: Shiv Sena (Eknath Shinde faction) corporator Ramesh Mhatre has been arrested for the assault on doctors and other medical staff.

title: No Evidence Linking Indian Officials To Nijjar Killing: Canada
content: Under a coordinated hunt named 'Operation Hardball', the law enforcement agencies of the US, Canada and Europe arrested 24 people for their alleged links to three organised crime groups, including the Bishnoi gang.

title: Several Feared Trapped As Under-Construction Building Collapses In Delhi
content: Two people were pulled out of the rubble as police, NDRF, and other emergency teams rushed to the site.

title: Sena Corporator Who Assaulted Doctor, Staff At Thane Hospital Arrested
content: Police registered a case against Ramesh Mhatre and five others on Tuesday night. He was taken into custody from his residence on Wednesday.

title: Spouse Can Seek Call And Hotel Records To Prove Adultery: Supreme Court
content: The case stems from a divorce petition filed by the wife, who accused her husband of cruelty and adultery.

title: "Going To Hit Them Hard Tonight": Trump Warns Iran After Declaring Deal "Over"
content: "We hit them very hard last night. Very, very hard. We'll probably hit them hard again tonight. I'll give them a little warning. We're going to hit them hard tonight," US President Donald Trump said while speaking during a NATO Summit appearance in Ankara, Turkey.

title: Qatari LNG Tanker, Struck Near Strait Of Hormuz, Awaits Salvage
content: All the crew have been safely evacuated. The LNG stored in the tanker's tanks remains intact and there is no breach of those tanks

title: PM To Hand Over Keys To 10,000 Dharavi Homes Within 18 Months: D Fadnavis
content: Speaking in the Assembly, Fadnavis said the project was not merely a housing scheme but an initiative aimed at transforming both living conditions and economic activity in Dharavi.

title: Sourav Ganguly set to be inducted in ICC Hall of Fame
content: sourav Ganguly is set to be inducted in the ICC's Hall of Famers Hall of Fame on July 11 . the former india captain will join an elite list of Indian cricketers . he will also join a former women's captain .

title: Desi doctor who drove his family off a cliff in California walks free after therapy
content: the 45-year-old radiologist lived with his wife and two children in a quiet cul-de-sac in Pasadena, California . prosecutors say he deliberately drove his Tesla off a 250-foot cliff at Devil's Slide, a notorious stretch of California's Pacific Coast Highway . all four survived, and a judge dismissed all three attempted murder charges .

title: History at Wimbledon: Arnav Paparkar becomes 1st Indian in 36 years to ...
content: Prajwal Hegde, Senior Editor (Tennis) at The Times of India since July 2005,  has covered all four Grand Slams—Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and US Open—for over a decade, along with Tour events across Asia and Europe, Davis Cup, and BJK Cup. She received the 2021 Ron Bookman Media Excellence Award from the ATP. Prajwal serves on the International Tennis Federation’s Media Commission and is a member of the International Tennis Writers Association. She appears in the docuseries Break Point and authored the Steffi Graf chapter in Sportstars 40, published by The Hindu in January 2020. Read More

title: The war that won't end: Why Iran ceasefire was always destined to fail

title: US charges Bishnoi, Brar in Nijjar murder, stops short of naming India
content: alias goldy brar charged with ordering the assassination of Hardeep Singh Nijjar . but neither the indictment nor US officials accused the government of India or any Indian official of directing, facilitating or participating in the killing . the distinction is significant because the murder precipitated one of the worst diplomatic crises in India-Canada relations .
